Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Fungal Electronics (2021) 48 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Physicist Astrid Eichhorn is a leader in the field of asymptotic safety 95 upvotes, 14 comments
  3. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Britain is ejecting hereditary nobles from Parliament after 700 years 27 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Temporal: A nine-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 405 upvotes, 140 comments
  6. Show HN: I built an ISP infrastructure emulator from scratch with a custom vBNG 43 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans. 1649 upvotes, 655 comments
  8. Show HN: Open-source browser for AI agents 82 upvotes, 24 comments
  9. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 103 upvotes, 32 comments
  10. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 313 upvotes, 121 comments
  11. How we hacked McKinsey's AI platform 353 upvotes, 140 comments
  12. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 199 upvotes, 80 comments
  13. BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 274 upvotes, 136 comments
  14. Show HN: Satellite imagery object detection using text prompts 29 upvotes, 11 comments
  15. The dead Internet is not a theory anymore 247 upvotes, 153 comments
  16. Show HN: Vanilla JavaScript refinery simulator built to explain job to my kids 72 upvotes, 37 comments
  17. Launch HN: Sentrial (YC W26) – Catch AI agent failures before your users do 20 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. 5,200 holes carved into a Peruvian mountain left by an ancient economy 65 upvotes, 35 comments
  19. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 96 upvotes, 56 comments
  20. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 174 upvotes, 113 comments
  21. Personal Computer by Perplexity 41 upvotes, 20 comments
  22. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 238 upvotes, 164 comments
  23. Launch HN: Prism (YC X25) – Workspace and API to generate and edit videos 27 upvotes, 14 comments
  24. Lego's 0.002mm specification and its implications for manufacturing (2025) 324 upvotes, 279 comments
  25. I'm glad the Anthropic fight is happening now 80 upvotes, 63 comments
  26. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 55 upvotes, 45 comments
  27. What Is a Tort? 19 upvotes, 18 comments
  28. The MacBook Neo 292 upvotes, 491 comments
  29. Swiss e-voting pilot can't count 2,048 ballots after decryption failure 117 upvotes, 277 comments
  30. Meticulous (YC S21) is hiring to redefine software dev 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 12 March 2026. Source here.
